summary: PLOS Wrap-up Phase backend testing complete — 18/18 tests PASSED (100%). All new endpoints (/snapshot-fusion, /monthly-summary(-ies), /alert-settings, /alert-history, /pregen/trigger-now, /pregen/log) return correct shape and data. Sandbox seed values match spec (income_30d~6165, expenses_30d~2296, assets~7047.50, liabilities~410). Recommended source correctly resolves to 'plaid' when items connected. Alert-settings default doc auto-created, PUT patch correctly filters non-allow-listed fields (bogus_field ignored). Pregen /trigger-now returns 4 tasks_completed (categorize, cashflow_forecast, monthly_summary, fraud_scan) with no ObjectId serialization errors. APScheduler registered at server startup (backend log shows '[pregen] scheduler started — next run: 2026-07-04 06:00:00-04:00'). All 8 Phase 1-5 regression endpoints still return 200. Frontend files (MonthlySummaryCard.tsx, alert-settings.tsx) lint clean (only cosmetic unused-var warnings). ONE non-blocking bug found: Claude AI narrative silently falls back to templated version because monthly_summary._claude_narrative() calls .with_max_tokens(2000) which does not exist on emergentintegrations LlmChat — the fallback path is exercised and works, but the Claude path never runs even when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set.

minor.issue: monthly_summary._claude_narrative() calls chat.with_max_tokens(2000) which raises AttributeError: 'LlmChat' object has no attribute 'with_max_tokens'. Exception is caught and fallback narrative is returned instead — visible in backend.err.log: 'Monthly narrative Claude call failed: LlmChat object has no attribute with_max_tokens'. Result: Claude is NEVER called even when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is present; users always get the shorter templated fallback. Fix: remove the .with_max_tokens(2000) chain, or pass max_tokens through send_message() per current emergentintegrations API. File: /app/backend/monthly_summary.py:161

action_items: Fix Claude narrative call in /app/backend/monthly_summary.py:161 — remove or replace .with_max_tokens(2000) so the Claude path actually executes when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set. Current behavior silently degrades to the fallback narrative for every user.

(Cosmetic) Rename unused catch variable `_e` to a leading-underscore convention that eslint accepts, or drop it, in: /app/frontend/src/components/MonthlySummaryCard.tsx:18, /app/frontend/app/finance/alert-settings.tsx:35, :47.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 6404 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline and should be split into feature-scoped routers.
monthly_summary.py properly handles the Claude failure with a graceful fallback (good defensive coding). However the fallback is masking a real bug (see minor issue #1).
pregen_scheduler.run_morning_pregen correctly pops '_id' before returning to avoid ObjectId serialization — verified by /pregen/trigger-now now returning 200.
alert-settings PUT properly uses an allow-list; verified bogus_field is stripped.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Existing sandbox seed preserved (not deleted on teardown per playbook contract). alert_settings and monthly_summaries collections were touched idempotently via normal endpoint use.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_plos_wrap_up.py is idempotent and can be re-run at any time. It loads /app/frontend/.env explicitly for EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL and uses test1@plos.app / test123. Sandbox seed is only created if missing; nothing is deleted on teardown. Scheduler verification uses grep -F on /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for '[pregen] scheduler started' string — will still work after any backend restart.
rca_of_issue: Claude narrative bug RCA: emergentintegrations.llm.chat.LlmChat exposes .with_model(...) but not .with_max_tokens(...). Calling it raises AttributeError which is caught by the broad try/except in _claude_narrative(), returning _fallback_narrative(). The fallback text starts with 'In {month_label} you earned $X...' — this is what every user sees, not a real Claude response. Reproduce: GET /api/plaid/monthly-summary?refresh=true and check backend.err.log for the WARNING. Mitigation: replace `.with_model('anthropic', 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929').with_max_tokens(2000)` with just `.with_model('anthropic', 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929')` — or if max tokens must be set, refer to the current emergentintegrations API docs.
